summary: Make M4 geometry-delta identity stable for M6 Color-encoded 3MF renders and preserve multipart metadata across identity attachment and disk-cache round trips.

externalInputs.purpose: Confirm from the official 3MF Consortium specification that build items and component references carry row-major affine transforms and that build transforms affect output geometry. No product or lookalike editor source was consulted.

decisions.decision: Hash a canonical 3MF geometry graph that retains mesh/topology, component and build references, and transforms while excluding ZIP packaging, comments, generated metadata and UUIDs, names, and material/color presentation. Perform archive decode and canonicalization in a dedicated one-shot worker using one combined rewrite pass; refuse large main-thread fallback. Preserve all existing mesh fields when replacing an engine-provided identity, and fail honestly without an identity if the 3MF cannot be canonicalized.
decisions.rationale: The M6 native engine emits a fresh 3MF for every 3D render. Exact pinned-engine evidence proved bytewise archive and model-XML hashes change across identical renders solely because creation timestamps and production UUIDs change. Hashing those containers violates AC-15.b by reporting a comment-only edit as geometry change. Geometry transforms must remain identity-bearing because Q-0035 records signed position deltas; names and colors must not invent geometric change under N-7. Keeping bounded large-model canonicalization off the renderer thread avoids turning the fix into a UI stall.

decisions.topic: Multipart metadata through the render disk cache
decisions.decision: Round-trip strictly validated contiguous multipart records through the optional disk cache and reject malformed part inventories instead of silently dropping or trusting them. Parts require a positive safe stats.triangles total, unique IDs, strict colors, contiguous safe ranges, positive safe counts, and an exact cumulative-total match.
decisions.rationale: Color and part visibility are shipped render-result behavior. Identity attachment and disk-cache decoding both removed the parts field, so a cache hit could present less product state than the engine result. Retaining validated parts makes cached and uncached presentations behaviorally equivalent.

testEvidence.criterion: The exact pinned engine must produce one semantic identity across repeated unchanged 3MF renders even when its containers differ.
testEvidence.test: Render the same checked-in stock-round-trip.scad fixture twice with the exact verified OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 executable, compare raw archive/model hashes, and hash both payloads through the new canonicalizer.

red.command: openscad.exe --export-format 3mf --backend Manifold --enable lazy-union -O export-3mf/color-mode=model -O export-3mf/material-type=color -o first.3mf stock-round-trip.scad; repeat to second.3mf
red.outcome: RED diagnostic: both renders reported the same six-facet geometry, but the raw 3MF archives and 3D/3dmodel.model payloads had different SHA-256 values; the XML diff was limited to CreationDate and generated production UUIDs.

green.outcome: GREEN: both exact-engine archives canonicalized to sha256:74508cb3367bdce6ddb507a66c1a39164b4d69a8ec048056294f4a4028e3bbb9. The temporary diagnostic script was removed after the bounded probe.

testEvidence.criterion: Cosmetic or container-only changes must not alter 3MF geometry identity, while coordinate and build-transform changes must.
testEvidence.test: Hash equivalent synthetic 3MF archives with different entry order/compression, timestamps, UUIDs, names, and colors; then change one coordinate and one build transform.

red.outcome: RED: 1 of 3 tests failed because raw ZIP SHA-256 values differed for identical triangle geometry.

green.outcome: GREEN after review corrections: all 29 focused geometry, worker-boundary, 3MF parser, and disk-cache tests passed; presentation-only variants matched, coordinate/transform variants differed, worker failures and compressed expansion stayed bounded, and malformed part inventories were rejected.

testEvidence.criterion: Multipart render metadata must survive disk-cache persistence and identity attachment.
testEvidence.test: Persist a render carrying one validated part, retrieve it twice through the cloned disk-cache path, and compare the complete result.

red.outcome: RED: the round-trip result lacked mesh.parts while the other ten cache tests passed.

green.outcome: GREEN after review corrections: TypeScript passed and all 29 focused tests passed with complete valid part metadata preserved and malformed inventories removed.

testEvidence.criterion: Large 3MF canonicalization must not block the renderer thread or allow unbounded main-thread fallback.
testEvidence.test: Exercise a supplied worker boundary for transfer/response/retirement behavior, reject malformed responses, and attempt a greater-than-1-MiB fallback with Worker unavailable.

red.command: Bounded read-only pre-commit review of the semantic-identity diff
red.outcome: RED review: synchronous unzip/decode plus repeated whole-model rewrites could freeze the renderer and multiply memory for the 2M-triangle baseline.

green.outcome: GREEN: 29 focused tests and TypeScript passed; production canonicalization is one combined pass in a one-shot worker, the original archive remains attached to the render result, workers always retire, main-thread fallback refuses archives over 1 MiB, and a highly compressed model expanding past the independent 8 MiB fallback cap is rejected before decompression.

questions: Q-0042 remains the only literal M6 specification contradiction and is unaffected by semantic geometry identity.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
